Program_Description: The coalition brings community members, OB practitioners, and other service providers together to discuss health outcomes, provide education, strategize care coordination, identify gaps in services, and strengthen relationships regarding the perinatal population.
Target_Population: Perinatal population including pregnant women and new parents

Program_Description: The Women, Infants & Children (WIC) Supplemental Nutrition Program is for income-eligible pregnant, breastfeeding, postpartum women, and children under the age of 5 who have a nutritional need.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children under 5
Eligibility_Requirements: Families who have incomes at 185% or less of the federal poverty level or receive Medi-Cal, CalWORKs (TANF), or CalFresh (SNAP) benefits; participants must reside in California.

Program_Description: The goal of the Perinatal Services Program is to improve the health of low-income pregnant women and give their babies a healthy start in life.
Special Supplemental Nutrition Program for Women, Infants, and Children.
Target_Population: Low-income pregnant, postpartum, and breastfeeding women; infants and children.

Program_Description: Resources for postpartum mental health support.
Target_Population: Pregnant and postpartum individuals experiencing mental health challenges.

Program_Description: Program for expectant parents and families with children from birth through age 3.
